req.title: Manager views document
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to view a document through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) a happy-path where an authenticated manager successfully views a document, receives HTTP 200/201, and an audit record is written; (2) negative paths covering unauthenticated access (401) and forbidden role (403), plus a validation-error path (400); (3) a boundary case exercising the minimum-length mandatory field value (exactly 1 character). Heuristics applied: EP for the normal manager flow, NEG for auth/role failures and validation errors, BVA for the 1-character boundary on a mandatory field, STC for the session-state transition from unauthenticated to authenticated, and DT for the role-permission matrix.

tests.name: test_manager_views_document_success
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
actions: POST /documents with a fully valid payload using manager credentials
Assert HTTP 200 or 201 is returned
Assert response body contains an entity id
Assert audit record is written for the operation
expected: Audit log contains one record referencing the returned entity id
trace: R-WEB-032: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_manager_views_document_success(manager_client, valid_document_payload, audit_log_reader):
    response = manager_client.post("/documents", json=valid_document_payload)
    assert response.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Expected 200 or 201, got {response.status_code}"
    )
    body = response.json()
    entity_id = body.get("id")
    assert entity_id, "Response must contain a non-empty entity id"
    audit_entries = audit_log_reader.fetch(entity_id=entity_id)
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1, (
        f"Expected at least one audit record for id={entity_id}"
    )

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_document
preconditions: No authentication token is present in the request
actions: POST /documents with a valid payload but no auth credentials
Assert HTTP 401 is returned
expected: Response body indicates authentication is required
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_document(anonymous_client, valid_document_payload, audit_log_reader):
    response = anonymous_client.post("/documents", json=valid_document_payload)
    assert response.status_code == 401, (
        f"Expected 401, got {response.status_code}"
    )
    body = response.json()
    assert "error" in body or "detail" in body, (
        "Response must carry an error description"
    )
    audit_entries = audit_log_reader.fetch_recent(seconds=5)
    assert len(audit_entries) == 0, (
        "No audit record should be written for unauthenticated requests"
    )

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_cannot_view_document
preconditions: User is authenticated but has role 'employee' (not manager)
actions: POST /documents with a valid payload using employee credentials
Assert HTTP 403 is returned
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_forbidden_role_cannot_view_document(employee_client, valid_document_payload, audit_log_reader):
    response = employee_client.post("/documents", json=valid_document_payload)
    assert response.status_code == 403, (
        f"Expected 403, got {response.status_code}"
    )
    body = response.json()
    assert "error" in body or "detail" in body, (
        "Response must carry a permission-denied description"
    )
    audit_entries = audit_log_reader.fetch_recent(seconds=5)
    assert len(audit_entries) == 0, (
        "No audit record should be written for forbidden role requests"
    )

tests.name: test_view_document_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
Payload is missing a mandatory field (e.g. 'title' is absent)
actions: Build a document payload omitting the mandatory 'title' field
POST /documents with the incomplete payload using manager credentials
Assert HTTP 400 is returned
expected: Response body references the missing mandatory field
trace: R-WEB-032: mandatory fields must be validated

tests.name: test_view_document_title_minimum_length_boundary
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
Title field minimum allowed length is 1 character
actions: Build a document payload where 'title' is exactly 1 character
POST /documents using manager credentials
Assert HTTP 200 or 201 is returned
Assert response body contains entity id
Assert audit record is written
expected: Audit log contains one record for the returned entity id
trace: R-WEB-032: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_view_document_title_minimum_length_boundary(manager_client, valid_document_payload, audit_log_reader):
    boundary_payload = dict(valid_document_payload)
    boundary_payload["title"] = "A"
    response = manager_client.post("/documents", json=boundary_payload)
    assert response.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Expected 200 or 201 for 1-char title, got {response.status_code}"
    )
    body = response.json()
    entity_id = body.get("id")
    assert entity_id, "Response must contain a non-empty entity id"
    audit_entries = audit_log_reader.fetch(entity_id=entity_id)
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1, (
        f"Expected audit record for boundary title, id={entity_id}"
    )

tests.name: test_session_state_transition_login_then_view_document
preconditions: Manager credentials exist but no session is active at test start
actions: Start from unauthenticated state; confirm /documents returns 401
Authenticate as manager via login endpoint to obtain a session token
POST /documents with the session token and a valid payload
Assert HTTP 200 or 201 and entity id in response
Assert audit record is written
expected: Audit record exists after successful document view
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_session_state_transition_login_then_view_document(anonymous_client, manager_credentials, valid_document_payload, audit_log_reader, make_authenticated_client):
    pre_response = anonymous_client.post("/documents", json=valid_document_payload)
    assert pre_response.status_code == 401, (
        f"Pre-login: expected 401, got {pre_response.status_code}"
    )
    authed_client = make_authenticated_client(manager_credentials)
    post_response = authed_client.post("/documents", json=valid_document_payload)
    assert post_response.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Post-login: expected 200 or 201, got {post_response.status_code}"
    )
    body = post_response.json()
    entity_id = body.get("id")
    assert entity_id, "Response must contain a non-empty entity id"
    audit_entries = audit_log_reader.fetch(entity_id=entity_id)
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1, (
        f"Expected audit record after state transition, id={entity_id}"
    )
